name: Sweep AI
pricing_notes: Free: 5 issues/mo; Pro $19/mo (unlimited); Enterprise custom; self-hosted available

auth.notes: GitHub App installation; Sweep authenticates via GitHub OAuth, no separate API key needed

performance.notes: Issue → PR turnaround: 2-10 minutes depending on complexity; background async processing

agent_readiness.notes: GitHub-native AI SWE agent. Create a GitHub issue tagged 'sweep' and it automatically creates a PR fixing the issue. Reads codebase, creates implementation plan, writes code, creates PR. Good for small-medium bug fixes and features. Self-hosted option. Less capable than Devin/OpenHands for complex multi-day tasks.

gotchas: Success rate varies significantly by task complexity — works best on isolated, well-defined issues
GitHub-only — not usable with GitLab, Bitbucket, or other hosting
Complex architectural changes often fail or require heavy human editing
best_when: Small bug fixes and well-scoped feature additions via GitHub Issues
Teams wanting automated PR creation from issue descriptions
GitHub-native workflows where Sweep can read codebase and create PRs
Developer time savings on routine tasks (fix typo, update dependency)
avoid_when: Complex architectural changes requiring multi-day context
Production-critical changes where hallucinated code is dangerous
Teams needing programmatic API integration (no REST API)
summary: Sweep is a GitHub-native AI SWE agent that creates PRs from GitHub Issues. Tag an issue 'sweep', and it reads your codebase, creates an implementation plan, and submits a PR. Best for isolated bug fixes and small features. GitHub-only. Free tier is 5 issues/month. Not suitable for complex architectural tasks. The most frictionless way to try AI-assisted coding for GitHub-native teams.
